heroHeadline: Trump Rejects Iran's Hormuz Proposal; UAE Quits OPEC; Brent $111 — Deadlock Deepens at Day 61
heroSubtitle: President Trump rejected Iran's April 26 Hormuz reopening proposal on April 28 — Day 60 — telling aides he was 'not satisfied' because it omitted nuclear terms, and directing preparation of an 'extended blockade.' Trump's claim that Iran had informed the US it was in 'a State of Collapse' was immediately contradicted on-air by CBS News' Tehran correspondent. The UAE announced its departure from OPEC effective May 1 in the largest defection in the cartel's six-decade history, as Brent crude topped $111.49/bbl and WTI crossed $100 for the first time since April 10 — the World Bank labeling the Hormuz closure 'the largest oil supply shock on record.' On Day 61 (April 29), Trump issued a 'no more Mr. Nice Guy' ultimatum to Tehran while Iran's FM Araghchi, speaking from Saint Petersburg after meeting Putin, declared Iran was 'reassessing its diplomatic approach' and described the Islamic Republic as 'stable, solid and powerful.' In Lebanon, the IDF destroyed Hezbollah's largest ever tunnel network in the Qantara area — seven people killed including emergency workers. The ceasefire on Iranian territory holds nominally, but the dual Hormuz blockade, financial warfare, and Lebanon front all continued to intensify with no resolution in sight.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-29. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, NBC News, Euronews, UN News, Axios, Washington Post, Army Recognition, France 24, SOF News, Pakistan Today, Iran Wire, Voice of Emirates, The New York Times, The Guardian, The Japan Times, Critical Threats, US News, House of Commons Library, World Bank, Jerusalem Post, UPI, JNS, ABC7, Windward Maritime Intelligence.
